message:
feat(ufunc): propagate integer sidecar through where_select for exact large-integer fidelity

The where_select operation previously discarded integer sidecar data,
returning `integer_sidecar: None` in the output array. This caused
silent precision loss for I64/U64 values exceeding f64's 53-bit
mantissa (e.g., 9_007_199_254_740_993).

Changes:
- Synthesize integer sidecars from both x and y inputs when the
  promoted output dtype is I64 or U64.
- Per-element, select the sidecar value from the branch chosen by
  the condition mask, with cross-type (I64<->U64) conversion and
  range-check error reporting.
- Fall back to ensure_exact_integer_bridge_value_supported when the
  selected branch lacks a sidecar (plain f64 source).
- Propagate the assembled sidecar into the output UFuncArray.
- Add test: where_select_preserves_large_u64_sidecar_values
  confirming round-trip of values beyond f64 precision.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
